questions.question: Which sentence uses a comma correctly?
answers: I went to the store, and I bought milk.
I went, to the store and I bought milk.
I, went to the store and I bought milk.
I went to the store and, I bought milk.
questions.explanation: Use a comma before a coordinating conjunction joining two independent clauses.

questions.question: Which sentence has correct subject-verb agreement?
answers: The dogs runs in the park.
The dogs run in the park.
The dogs is running in the park.
The dogs were runs in the park.
questions.explanation: The plural subject 'dogs' requires the plural verb 'run'.

questions.question: Which is the correct plural of 'child'?
questions.explanation: The irregular plural of 'child' is 'children'.

questions.question: Which sentence is in the active voice?
answers: The cake was eaten by the children.
questions.explanation: Active voice: subject performs the action directly.

questions.question: Which word is a conjunction?
questions.explanation: 'Although' is a subordinating conjunction.

questions.question: Which sentence uses an apostrophe correctly?
questions.explanation: Use 's to show singular possession.

questions.question: What type of sentence is this: 'Run!'
questions.explanation: An imperative sentence gives a command.

questions.question: Which word is an adverb in: 'She sings beautifully'?
questions.explanation: Beautifully modifies the verb 'sings' — it's an adverb.

questions.question: Which sentence is punctuated correctly?
questions.explanation: An interjection like 'Wow' is followed by a comma, then the rest of the sentence.

questions.question: Which word correctly completes: 'Neither the teacher ___ the students were late.'?
questions.explanation: 'Neither...nor' is a correlative conjunction pair.

questions.question: What is the correct past tense of 'bring'?
questions.explanation: The irregular past tense of 'bring' is 'brought'.

questions.question: Which sentence uses 'its' correctly?
questions.explanation: 'Its' (no apostrophe) is the possessive pronoun; 'it's' means 'it is'.

questions.question: What is a compound sentence?
answers: A sentence with one independent clause
A sentence joined by a coordinating conjunction
A sentence with only a dependent clause
questions.explanation: A compound sentence joins two independent clauses with a coordinating conjunction.

questions.question: Which word is a preposition in: 'The book is on the table'?
questions.explanation: 'On' is a preposition showing the relationship between the book and the table.

questions.question: Which sentence uses the correct form of 'lie/lay'?
answers: I need to lay down for a nap.
I need to lie down for a nap.
I need to laying down for a nap.
I need to lied down for a nap.
questions.explanation: 'Lie' means to recline; 'lay' requires an object.

questions.question: Which sentence correctly uses a semicolon?
answers: I love pizza; because it is delicious.
I love pizza; it is my favorite food.
I; love pizza it is delicious.
I love; pizza it is delicious.
questions.explanation: A semicolon connects two closely related independent clauses.

questions.question: What part of speech is 'beautiful' in: 'The beautiful sunset amazed us'?
questions.explanation: 'Beautiful' modifies the noun 'sunset', so it is an adjective.

questions.question: Which sentence avoids a double negative?
questions.explanation: Double negatives cancel each other out. Use 'anything' with 'didn't'.

questions.question: Which homophone correctly completes: 'I did not ___ the answer.'?
questions.explanation: 'Know' means to be aware of something; 'no' is a negative response.

questions.question: What punctuation ends an interrogative sentence?
questions.explanation: An interrogative sentence asks a question and ends with a question mark.

minigame_comma.sentence: Before you leave please turn off the lights and close the door.
minigame_comma.correct: Before you leave, please turn off the lights, and close the door.
choices: Before you leave please turn off the lights and close the door.
Before you leave, please turn off the lights, and close the door.
Before, you leave please turn off the lights and close, the door.
Before you leave please, turn off the lights and close the door.

minigame_comma.sentence: My favorite foods are pizza tacos and sushi.
minigame_comma.correct: My favorite foods are pizza, tacos, and sushi.
choices: My favorite foods are pizza tacos and sushi.
My, favorite foods are pizza tacos and sushi.
My favorite foods are pizza, tacos, and sushi.
My favorite foods, are pizza tacos and sushi.

minigame_comma.sentence: Although it was raining we decided to go for a walk.
minigame_comma.correct: Although it was raining, we decided to go for a walk.
choices: Although it was raining we decided to go for a walk.
Although, it was raining we decided to go for a walk.
Although it was raining, we decided to go for a walk.
Although it was raining we, decided to go for a walk.

minigame_comma.sentence: The tall dark and handsome stranger walked into the room.
minigame_comma.correct: The tall, dark, and handsome stranger walked into the room.
choices: The tall dark and handsome stranger walked into the room.
The tall, dark, and handsome stranger walked into the room.
The, tall dark and handsome stranger walked into the room.
The tall, dark and handsome, stranger walked into the room.

minigame_comma.sentence: Yes I would love to come to your birthday party.
minigame_comma.correct: Yes, I would love to come to your birthday party.
choices: Yes I would love to come to your birthday party.
Yes, I would love to come to your birthday party.
Yes I, would love to come to your birthday party.
Yes I would love, to come to your birthday party.

minigame_comma.sentence: We visited Paris France last summer.
minigame_comma.correct: We visited Paris, France last summer.
choices: We visited Paris France last summer.
We visited Paris France, last summer.
We visited Paris, France last summer.
We visited, Paris France last summer.

minigame_comma.sentence: The cat however refused to come inside.
minigame_comma.correct: The cat, however, refused to come inside.
choices: The cat however refused to come inside.
The cat, however refused to come inside.
The, cat however, refused to come inside.
The cat, however, refused to come inside.

minigame_comma.sentence: I wanted to go swimming but it was too cold outside.
minigame_comma.correct: I wanted to go swimming, but it was too cold outside.
choices: I wanted to go swimming but it was too cold outside.
I, wanted to go swimming but it was too cold outside.
I wanted to go swimming, but it was too cold outside.
I wanted to go swimming but, it was too cold outside.

minigame_comma.sentence: On Monday March 15 2024 the new library will open.
minigame_comma.correct: On Monday, March 15, 2024, the new library will open.
choices: On Monday March 15 2024 the new library will open.
On Monday, March 15, 2024, the new library will open.
On, Monday March 15 2024 the new library will open.
On Monday March, 15, 2024 the new library will open.

minigame_comma.sentence: Maria who is my best friend just moved to a new city.
minigame_comma.correct: Maria, who is my best friend, just moved to a new city.
choices: Maria who is my best friend just moved to a new city.
Maria, who is my best friend just moved to a new city.
Maria, who is my best friend, just moved to a new city.
Maria who is, my best friend, just moved to a new city.

minigame_pos.sentence: She ran quickly to catch the bus.
The brilliant student aced every test.
She visited the library every week.
He climbed the tall mountain with ease.
The joyful child laughed all day.
She whispered a secret into his ear.
The children sat silently during the test.
The mountain was covered in snow.
An enormous whale surfaced near the boat.
They danced under the stars all evening.
The firefighter bravely entered the burning building.
Friendship is one of life's greatest gifts.
